channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Terrion Arnold still in custody despite approved bond in pending felony case
item.description: Terrion Arnold, a professional football player, continues to be held in jail even though a judge approved his request for release on bond the previous day in connection with multiple felony charges ti...

item.title: Katy Tur comments on perceived changes in Donald Trump's speech patterns over time
item.description: In a recent episode of The Daily Beast Podcast, journalist Katy Tur discussed her observations regarding former President Donald Trump’s speaking style and perceived changes in his communication over ...

item.title: El-Rufai bail ruling delayed after judge falls ill in Kaduna court
item.description: The Federal High Court in Kaduna was unable to proceed with its scheduled sitting on Wednesday after the presiding judge, Justice Hauwa’u Buhari, reportedly fell ill. This development led to a delay i...

item.title: CNN Anchor Debates MAGA Correspondent on Birth Tourism Concerns and Birthright Citizenship
item.description: In a heated CNN panel discussion, anchor Abby Phillip challenged a New York Post correspondent defending MAGA positions on immigration and birthright citizenship. The debate was sparked by comments fr...

item.title: Russian missile and drone strikes on Kyiv leave over a dozen dead and dozens injured, officials say
item.description: A large-scale Russian aerial assault on Kyiv overnight into Thursday resulted in significant casualties and widespread destruction, according to Ukrainian officials. Kyiv Mayor Vitaliy Klitschko repor...

item.title: Olivia de Havilland’s Career Transformation and Life in the 1950s
item.description: This article explores the life and career of Dame Olivia de Havilland during the 1950s, a pivotal decade in which she reinvented herself both professionally and personally. Already an acclaimed Hollyw...

item.title: DOJ whistleblower asks Senate to review compliance with federal records laws by Todd Blanche
item.description: A whistleblower from the U.S. Department of Justice is calling on the Senate Judiciary Committee to investigate whether Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche complied with federal records laws during h...

item.title: Colorado governor removes clemency board members after dispute over Tina Peters commutation process
item.description: Colorado Governor Jared Polis has dismissed two members of the state’s clemency board after they publicly criticized his decision to grant clemency to Tina Peters, a former county clerk convicted in c...

item.title: 1951 Atlas Babycar 2S: Rare French Post-War Microcar Design
item.description: The 1951 Atlas Babycar 2S is a rare and unusual microcar produced in post-World War II France, reflecting a period when European manufacturers experimented with compact, economical transportation solu...

item.title: EPA Documerica Photographs Document Pollution and Daily Life Along the Ohio River in the 1970s
item.description: In the early 1970s, the Ohio River stood at a crossroads between industrial prosperity and severe environmental degradation. Serving as a major commercial waterway for the Midwest, the river supported...

item.title: House Judiciary Hearing Turns Into Heated Exchange Between Rep. Lawler and Rep. Raskin Over Immigration-Related Violence
item.description: A House Judiciary subcommittee hearing on sanctuary policies devolved into a loud confrontation between Rep. Mike Lawler (R-NY) and Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-MD). The dispute centered on how lawmakers shou...

item.title: LensCulture Highlights Photography Projects Exploring LGBTQ+ Identity for Pride Month
item.description: LensCulture presents a curated Pride Month feature showcasing a selection of contemporary photography projects centered on LGBTQ+ identity, queerness, and self-expression. The editors emphasize their ...

item.title: Statue of Anubis Discovered in Tutankhamun’s Tomb During 1922 Carter Expedition
item.description: The statue of Anubis was uncovered in 1922 during Howard Carter’s archaeological expedition into the tomb of Pharaoh Tutankhamun, one of the most significant discoveries in Egyptology. Anubis, the anc...

item.title: House Rules Committee Blocks Vote on Amendment to Limit US-Israel Military Cooperation, Drawing Criticism
item.description: A Republican-controlled House committee has come under criticism after it declined to allow a floor vote on a bipartisan amendment related to U.S. military cooperation with Israel. The proposed amendm...

item.title: California Legislature Considers Early Math Assessments for Kindergarten Through Second Grade
item.description: California lawmakers are considering Senate Bill 1067, which would require public schools to assess students' foundational math skills from kindergarten through second grade beginning with the 2028-29...

item.title: A Look at Susan Hayward’s Swimsuit Publicity Portraits and Hollywood Style in the 1940s and 1950s
item.description: This article showcases a collection of 30 vintage photographs featuring Academy Award-winning actress Susan Hayward wearing swimsuits during the 1940s and 1950s while examining how the images reflecte...

item.title: Legal challenge targets Florida GOP candidate over residency requirements amid internal Republican backlash
item.description: A legal dispute has emerged within Florida Republican politics involving gubernatorial candidate James Fishback, a hedge fund manager and outspoken conservative figure. Florida Lt. Gov. Jay Collins fi...

item.title: Critics Condemn Trump’s Reaction to Supreme Court Birthright Citizenship Ruling
item.description: The article reports on widespread criticism of President Donald Trump following his response to a recent Supreme Court decision concerning birthright citizenship. The Court upheld the constitutional p...

item.title: House Republicans Block Mike Johnson's Attempt to Advance SAVE America Act Through Defense Bill
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson suffered a significant legislative setback after an attempt to advance the SAVE America Act by attaching it to the annual National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) failed du...

item.title: Jayne Mansfield’s 1957 Promotional Visit to Rotterdam Draws Public and Military Attention
item.description: On October 11, 1957, American actress Jayne Mansfield carried out a highly publicized promotional visit to Rotterdam in the Netherlands, creating a memorable moment that blended entertainment, media s...
